data.content:
### Initialize New Sandbox Template with SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/template/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox template using the E2B SDK. Allows specifying the path, template name, and target language (typescript, python-sync, python-async).

```bash
e2b template init-v2 [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.5/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version. It takes a message and an optional stack trace.

```TypeScript
class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
    if (stackTrace) {
      this.stack = stackTrace;
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.3.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version due to outdated envd usage. It requires a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string): TemplateError
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.0/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when a template uses an outdated version of envd, indicating incompatibility with the current SDK. This error inherits from SandboxError.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends SandboxError {
  message: string;

  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

The TemplateError is thrown when a template is incompatible with the current SDK version, typically due to using an older envd version. It includes a message and an optional stack trace, guiding users on necessary template updates.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: TemplateError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Indicates an error when a template uses an incompatible, older version of envd. It requires a string message.

```typescript
new TemplateError(message: string): TemplateError
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when a template uses an incompatible, older version of the envd environment. This error indicates a version mismatch issue in the E2B template system. It expects a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Define TemplateError

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template uses an incompatible old envd version. The constructor accepts a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    // ... implementation
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/errors/page.mdx

Represents an error when a template uses an incompatible or old version of the E2B environment. This error extends SandboxError and requires a string message.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends SandboxError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, thrown when a template uses an incompatible old envd version. It requires a string message for instantiation.

```ts
class TemplateError {
  constructor(message: string)
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This overload of the static `create` method allows for the instantiation of a new sandbox from a specific sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. It accepts the template identifier and optional connection options (`SandboxOpts`), returning a Promise that resolves to the created sandbox instance. An example illustrates how to use it with a template name.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, template: string, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

/**
 * Create a new sandbox from the specified sandbox template.
 *
 * @template S The type of the Sandbox class.
 * @param this The Sandbox class.
 * @param template The sandbox template name or ID.
 * @param opts Optional connection options.
 * @returns A Promise resolving to the sandbox instance for the new sandbox.
 */

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Template - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template. This method takes the template name or ID as a string argument and returns a Promise resolving to the sandbox instance. Optional connection options can also be provided.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a user-specified sandbox template. This method requires the template name or ID as a string argument and accepts optional connection options. It returns a Promise that resolves to the sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S,
   template: string,
   opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Code in E2B Sandbox (JavaScript/TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/README.md

Demonstrates how to create a sandbox instance, run code within it, and retrieve the execution results using the E2B JavaScript SDK. It requires the @e2b/code-interpreter package.

```typescript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
await sandbox.runCode('x = 1')

const execution = await sandbox.runCode('x+=1; x')
console.log(execution.text)  // outputs 2
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox from Specified Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Create a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. This method takes the template identifier and optional sandbox creation options, returning a promise that resolves to the new sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Default Template using TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Create a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template. This method is asynchronous and returns a promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template using TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Create a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. This method is asynchronous and returns a promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, template: string, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom Sandbox with Template ID (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Starts a new sandbox instance using a previously built custom template. The template ID is passed to the `Sandbox.create` method in JavaScript.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create({
  template: 'YOUR_TEMPLATE_ID',
})
```

--------------------------------

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, indicating incompatibility with older envd versions. It requires a string message during instantiation.

```typescript
class TemplateError {
  message: string;
  constructor(message: string) {
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

Creates a new sandbox instance. This method can create from a default 'base' template or a specified template, returning a sandbox instance upon successful creation.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template or a specified sandbox template. It returns a Promise that resolves to the sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S,
   template: string,
   opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Create Sandbox from Default Template - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.4.0/sandbox/page.mdx

The static `create` method, when called without a template argument, instantiates a new sandbox using the default `desktop` template. It is a generic method that takes optional connection options (`SandboxOpts`) and returns a Promise resolving to the created sandbox instance. An example demonstrates its usage.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

/**
 * Create a new sandbox from the default `desktop` sandbox template.
 *
 * @template S The type of the Sandbox class.
 * @param this The Sandbox class.
 * @param opts Optional connection options.
 * @returns A Promise resolving to the sandbox instance for the new sandbox.
 */

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

--------------------------------

### Define InvalidArgumentError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

The InvalidArgumentError is thrown when a function or method receives arguments that do not meet the expected criteria. It can optionally accept a stack trace string for debugging purposes. This error is crucial for maintaining data integrity within the SDK.

```typescript
class InvalidArgumentError {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.7.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template (by name or ID). Accepts optional connection options. Returns a Promise that resolves with the new sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v2.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specific sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. This allows for custom sandbox configurations. It returns a Promise that resolves with the created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static betaCreate<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxBetaCreateOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox (Beta) - Default Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox using the default 'base' sandbox template. This is a beta feature and may be subject to change. It returns a promise that resolves to an instance of the created sandbox.

```typescript
static betaCreate<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxBetaCreateOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.betaCreate()
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Run Command in Background

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background, returning a CommandHandle for later interaction. Use CommandHandle.wait to retrieve the final result. Takes the command string and optional start options.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Define InvalidArgumentError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.0/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when an invalid argument is provided to a function or method. This error inherits from SandboxError.

```typescript
class InvalidArgumentError extends SandboxError {
  message: string;

  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### CommandStartOpts Interface - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

The `CommandStartOpts` interface defines the configuration options for starting a new command. It includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, callbacks for stdout/stderr, request timeouts, command timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Some properties have default values.

```typescript
interface CommandStartOpts {
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox using TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Create a new sandbox instance. This function can create a sandbox from the default template or a specified template. It accepts optional sandbox creation options.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation Example (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

An example demonstrating how to create a new sandbox instance using the `Sandbox.create` method. Replace '<template-name-or-id>' with the actual template name or ID.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')

```

--------------------------------

### Configure Command Start Options in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/commands/page.mdx

Defines the options for starting a new command in the E2B environment. This includes settings for running commands in the background, specifying the working directory (cwd), environment variables (envs), and callbacks for standard error (onStderr) and standard output (onStdout). It also supports request and command timeouts, and specifying the user to run the command as.

```ts
{
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox Instance (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance, either from the default 'base' template or a specified template. The method returns a promise that resolves to the sandbox instance. Options for connection can be provided during creation.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S,
   template: string,
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

--------------------------------

### Commands Execution: Run Command

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.3/commands/page.mdx

Enables starting and managing command executions within the sandbox. It supports running commands to completion, returning results, or running them in the background, returning a handle for later interaction. Options like `background` control the execution mode. Dependencies include CommandStartOpts and object types.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult>
run(cmd: string, opts: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandHandle>
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult | CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.4/commands/page.mdx

Initiates a new command execution within the sandbox. This function allows configuration of various aspects such as running in the background, specifying the working directory, setting environment variables, and defining callbacks for standard output and error streams. It also supports request and command timeouts, and specifies the user to run the command as. Returns a CommandHandle for managing the started command.

```typescript
startCommand(command: string,
   args?: string[] | undefined,
   opts?: CommandStartOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Format Sandbox Timeout Error

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/errors/page.mdx

A function to format a sandbox timeout error, returning a TimeoutError object. It takes a string message as input.

```typescript
function formatSandboxTimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError {
  // ... implementation
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define InvalidArgumentError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when an invalid argument is provided to a function or method. This error is crucial for input validation within the E2B SDK, ensuring that operations receive correctly formatted data. It takes a string message.

```typescript
class InvalidArgumentError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "InvalidArgumentError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Run Command (Flexible Mode)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command with flexible options. If opts.background is true, returns a CommandHandle for background execution. Otherwise, waits for completion and returns a CommandResult.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult | CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Command Start Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.1/commands/page.mdx

Defines options for starting a new command within the E2B environment. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, stdout/stderr callbacks, request timeout, command timeout, and the user to run the command as.

```ts
optional background: boolean;

optional cwd: string;

optional envs: Record<string, string>;

optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;

optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;

optional requestTimeoutMs: number;

optional timeoutMs: number;

optional user: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Specified Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template, identified by its name or ID. This static method requires the 'Sandbox' class context and accepts optional connection options. It returns a Promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string,
   opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: InvalidArgumentError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Represents an error thrown when an invalid argument is provided to a function or method. It requires a string message.

```typescript
new InvalidArgumentError(message: string): InvalidArgumentError
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Default Template (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the default 'desktop' sandbox template. This method is static and requires the 'Sandbox' class context. It accepts optional connection options and returns a Promise that resolves to the sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

--------------------------------

### Command Start Options in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for initiating a new command. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, stdout/stderr callbacks, request timeouts, command timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Defaults are provided for several options.

```typescript
optional background: boolean;
```

```typescript
optional cwd: string;
// home directory of the user used to start the command
```

```typescript
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
// overrides the default environment variables from Sandbox constructor.
// Default: {}
```

```typescript
optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
```

```typescript
optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
```

```typescript
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
// Default: 30_000 // 30 seconds
```

```typescript
optional timeoutMs: number;
// Default: 60_000 // 60 seconds
```

```typescript
optional user: Username;
// Default: user
```

--------------------------------

### Set Working Directory for Command in E2B SDK

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.0/commands/page.mdx

This option specifies the working directory for a command. If not provided, the command will run in the home directory of the user who started it. This is part of the `CommandStartOpts` interface.

```typescript
optional cwd: string;
// Default: // home directory of the user used to start the command
```

--------------------------------

### Define TemplateError Class (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.2/errors/page.mdx

Defines the TemplateError class, inheriting from SandboxError. This error indicates that a template uses an incompatible, older version of envd. It requires a string message for context.

```typescript
class TemplateError extends SandboxError {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "TemplateError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define InvalidArgumentError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.5/errors/page.mdx

Defines the InvalidArgumentError class, used when function arguments are invalid. It accepts a message and an optional stack trace.

```TypeScript
class InvalidArgumentError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "InvalidArgumentError";
    if (stackTrace) {
      this.stack = stackTrace;
    }
  }
}
```

### TypeScript: TimeoutError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Represents various timeout scenarios, including sandbox timeouts, request timeouts, and command execution timeouts. It takes a string message.

```typescript
new TimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.5/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for starting a new command within the sandbox. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, standard error/output callbacks, request timeouts, command-specific timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Defaults are provided for most options.

```typescript
optional background: boolean;
optional cwd: string;
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
optional timeoutMs: number;
optional user: Username;
```

### Format Sandbox Timeout Error in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

A utility function to format and create a TimeoutError specifically for sandbox-related timeouts. It helps in standardizing how timeout errors are represented within the E2B SDK. It takes a string message and returns a TimeoutError object.

```typescript
function formatSandboxTimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError {
  return new TimeoutError(message);
}
```
